HomeSubtitle: Your clean Windows experience is just a few clicks away!
HomeUserQuestion: What user do you want to apply changes to?
AppRemovalSubtitle: Select which apps you want to remove from your system
AppPresetDefaultTooltip: Select the apps that are safe to remove for most users
AppPresetLastUsedTooltip: Select the apps that were removed the last time Win11Debloat was run
TweaksSubtitle: Select which tweaks you want to apply to your system, hover over settings for more information
TweaksPresetDefaultTooltip: Select the settings that are recommended for most people
TweaksPresetLastUsedTooltip: Select the settings that were used the last time Win11Debloat was run
TweaksPresetPrivacyTooltip: Select all Privacy & Suggested Content tweaks
TweaksDetectAppliedTooltip: Detect all tweaks currently applied for the current user.
DeploySubtitle: Configure how your changes will be applied and more
DeployInitialScopeDesc: Apps will be removed for all users and from the Windows image to prevent reinstallation for new users.
DeployRestartExplorer: Restart the Windows Explorer process to apply all changes immediately
AppScopeAllUsersDesc: Apps will be removed for all users and from the Windows image to prevent reinstallation for new users.
AppScopeCurrentUserDesc: Apps will only be removed for the current user.
AppScopeTargetUserDesc: Apps will only be removed for the specified target user.
UserDescOtherUser: A different user profile on this system
UserDescOtherUserNamed: A different user profile on this system: {0}
UserDescSysprep: The default user template, affecting all new users created after this point. Useful for Sysprep deployment.
MsgExportFailed: Unable to open export configuration dialog: {0}
MsgImportFailed: Unable to open import configuration dialog: {0}
MsgNoChangesSelected: No changes have been selected, please select at least one option to proceed.
TweaksCategoryWikiTooltip: Open wiki for more info on '{0}' tweaks
AppRecommendationSafe: [Recommended] Safe to remove for most users
AppRecommendationUnsafe: [Not Recommended] Only remove if you know what you are doing
AppRecommendationOptional: [Optional] Remove if you don't need this app
TweakDisabledWhenApplied: This tweak is already applied and cannot be undone automatically. Visit the Win11Debloat wiki for instructions on how to manually revert this change.

FeaturesToolTips.DisableTelemetry: This setting disables telemetry, diagnostic data collection, activity history, app-launch tracking, targeted ads and more. It limits the data that is sent to Microsoft about your device and usage. If you are a Windows Insider, updates may be blocked until optional diagnostic data collection is turned back on.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableSuggestions: This setting removes many annoying distractions from Windows. This includes things like notifications, reminders and sync provider ads. It also prevents automated installation of suggested apps.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableLocationServices: This will turn off Windows Location Services and deny apps access to your location. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableFindMyDevice: This will turn off the 'Find My Device' feature, which periodically sends your device's location to Microsoft. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableLockscreenTips: This will turn off the lockscreen spotlight option and disable the tips, tricks and fun facts that appear on the lock screen.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableDesktopSpotlight: This will turn off the 'Windows Spotlight' feature for the desktop background, which shows different background images and occasionally tips and fun facts on the desktop. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableEdgeAds: This will turn off various distractions from Microsoft Edge such as ads, suggestions and the MSN news feed. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableCopilot: This will disable and uninstall Microsoft Copilot, Windows' built-in AI assistant.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableRecall: This will disable Windows Recall, an AI-powered feature that provides quick access to recently used files, apps and activities. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableClickToDo: This will disable Click To Do, which provides AI-powered text and image analysis features in Windows. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableAISvcAutoStart: This will set the WSAIFabricSvc service to manual startup, preventing the service from starting automatically with Windows.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableDVR: This will disable the Xbox game/screen recording features included with the Game Bar app. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableGameBarIntegration: This will disable the Game Bar integration with games and controllers. This stops annoying ms-gamebar popups when launching games or connecting a controller.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableStartRecommended: This will hide the recommended section in the start menu, which shows recently added apps, recently opened files and app recommendations. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableStartAllApps: This will hide the 'All Apps' section in the start menu, which shows all installed apps. WARNING: Hiding this section may make it harder to find installed apps on your system. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableStartPhoneLink: This will remove the Phone Link integration in the start menu when you have a mobile device linked to your PC.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableBing: This will turn off Bing web search results and Copilot integration in the Windows search experience. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableStoreSearchSuggestions: This will disable the Microsoft Store app suggestions in Windows search.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableSettings365Ads: This will turn off the Microsoft 365 Copilot ads that appear in the Settings Home page.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableSettingsHome: Removes the 'Home' page from the Settings app.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableEdgeAI: This will turn off AI features in Microsoft Edge, such as the AI-powered sidebar and Copilot features. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.DisablePaintAI: This will turn off AI features in Paint, such as the AI-powered image generation and editing tools. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableNotepadAI: This will turn off AI features in Notepad, such as the AI-powered writing suggestions. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.EnableDarkMode: This will set the app and system theme to dark mode.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableDragTray: The Drag Tray is a new feature for sharing & moving files in Windows 11, it appears at the top of the screen when dragging files.
FeaturesToolTips.RevertContextMenu: This will restore the classic Windows 10 style context menu, which is normally hidden behind the 'Show more options' entry in the new Windows 11 context menu.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableMouseAcceleration: This will disable mouse acceleration which is enabled by default in Windows. This makes mouse movement more consistent and predictable.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableStickyKeys: This will prevent the Sticky Keys dialog from appearing when you press the Shift key 5 times in a row.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableWindowSnapping: This will turn off the ability to snap windows to the sides or corners of the screen.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableSnapAssist: This will turn off app suggestions when you snap windows to the sides or corners of the screen.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableSnapLayouts: This will turn off the snap layout flyout that appears when you hover over the maximize button or drag windows to the top of the screen.
FeaturesToolTips.TaskbarAlignLeft: By default, Windows 11 has the taskbar buttons centered. Enabling this setting will move the taskbar buttons to the left, similar to previous versions of Windows.
FeaturesToolTips.HideTaskview: This will disable the 'Task view' button on the taskbar, which allows you to see all your open windows and virtual desktops.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableWidgets: This will disable the widgets features in Windows, including the widgets button on the taskbar and the widgets that can appear on the lock screen.
FeaturesToolTips.HideChat: This will disable the Chat (meet now) icon on the taskbar.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableStorageSense: This will disable Storage Sense, which automatically frees up disk space by deleting temporary files, emptying the recycle bin and cleaning up files in the Downloads folder.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableFastStartup: Fast Start-up helps your PC start faster after shutdown by saving a system image to disk. Disabling Fast Start-up can help with certain issues, but may result in slightly longer boot times.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableBitlockerAutoEncryption: For devices that support it, Windows 11 automatically enables BitLocker device encryption. Disabling this will turn off automatic encryption of the device, but you can still manually enable BitLocker encryption if desired. Drives that are already encrypted with BitLocker will remain encrypted when this setting is applied.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableModernStandbyNetworking: By default, devices that support Modern Standby maintain network connectivity while in sleep mode. Disabling network connectivity during Modern Standby can help save battery life.
FeaturesToolTips.EnableEndTask: When enabled, adds an 'End Task' option to the right-click context menu for apps in the taskbar, allowing you to quickly force close apps.
FeaturesToolTips.EnableLastActiveClick: When enabled, clicking on an app in the taskbar will switch to the last active window of that app, instead of only showing the thumbnail preview.
FeaturesToolTips.ShowKnownFileExt: This will show file extensions for known file types. By default, Windows hides file extensions for known file types which can lead to confusion and security risks.
FeaturesToolTips.ShowHiddenFolders: By default, Windows hides certain files, folders and drives to prevent accidental modification or deletion. Turn this on to show all files in File Explorer.
FeaturesToolTips.HideDupliDrive: By default, Windows shows removable drives both under 'This PC' and in the navigation pane with its own entry. Enable this setting to only show removable drives under 'This PC'.
FeaturesToolTips.HideHome: Hides the 'Home' section from the File Explorer navigation pane.
FeaturesToolTips.HideGallery: Hides the 'Gallery' section from the File Explorer navigation pane.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableTransparency: This will disable transparency effects on Windows and interfaces. Which can help improve performance on older hardware.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableAnimations: This will disable animations and some visual effects in Windows, which can make the interface feel snappier, especially on older hardware.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableUpdateASAP: This will prevent your PC from being among the first to receive new non-security updates. Your PC will still receive these updates eventually.
FeaturesToolTips.PreventUpdateAutoReboot: This will prevent your PC from automatically restarting after updates while any user is signed in.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableDeliveryOptimization: This will prevent your PC from sharing downloaded updates with other PCs on the local network or on the internet. This also prevents your PC from downloading updates from other PCs.
FeaturesToolTips.HideOnedrive: Hides the 'OneDrive' section from the File Explorer navigation pane.
FeaturesToolTips.Hide3dObjects: Hides the '3D objects' folder from the File Explorer navigation pane.
FeaturesToolTips.HideMusic: Hides the 'Music' folder from the File Explorer navigation pane.
FeaturesToolTips.AddFoldersToThisPC: This setting will add common folders like Desktop, Documents, Downloads, Music, Pictures and Videos back to the 'This PC' page in File Explorer.
FeaturesToolTips.HideIncludeInLibrary: Hides the 'Include in library' option from the File Explorer context menu.
FeaturesToolTips.HideGiveAccessTo: Hides the 'Give access to' option from the File Explorer context menu.
FeaturesToolTips.HideShare: Hides the 'Share' option from the File Explorer context menu.
FeaturesToolTips.DisableBraveBloat: This will disable Brave's built-in AI features, Crypto wallet, News, Rewards, Talk and VPN. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.
FeaturesToolTips.EnableWindowsSandbox: Windows Sandbox is a lightweight desktop environment for safely running applications in isolation. Software installed inside the Windows Sandbox environment remains 'sandboxed' and runs separately from the host machine. Only supported on Windows 11 Pro, Workstation, and Enterprise editions.
FeaturesToolTips.EnableWindowsSubsystemForLinux: Windows Subsystem for Linux allows you to run a Linux environment directly on Windows without the need for a virtual machine.
FeaturesToolTips.ShowDriveLettersFirst: This setting will show drive letters before the drive label in File Explorer.
FeaturesToolTips.ShowDriveLettersLast: This setting will show drive letters after the drive label in File Explorer (Default Windows behavior).
FeaturesToolTips.ShowNetworkDriveLettersFirst: This setting will show only network drive letters before the drive label in File Explorer.
FeaturesToolTips.HideDriveLetters: This setting will hide all drive letters from the File Explorer navigation pane and 'This PC'.
FeaturesToolTips.StartAllAppsCategory: This will set the All Apps section in the start menu to show apps grouped by category.
FeaturesToolTips.StartAllAppsGrid: This will set the All Apps section in the start menu to show apps in an alphabetical grid layout.
FeaturesToolTips.StartAllAppsList: This will set the All Apps section in the start menu to show apps in an alphabetical list layout.

GroupsToolTips.SearchIcon: This setting allows you to customize the appearance of the search box on the taskbar.
GroupsToolTips.MultiMon: This setting allows you to choose where taskbar app buttons are shown when using multiple monitors.
GroupsToolTips.CombineButtons: This setting allows you to choose how taskbar buttons are combined on the main display.
GroupsToolTips.CombineMMButtons: This setting allows you to choose how taskbar buttons are combined on secondary displays.
GroupsToolTips.ClearStart: This setting allows you to quickly remove all pinned apps from the start menu.
GroupsToolTips.ExplorerLocation: This setting allows you to choose the default location that File Explorer opens to.
GroupsToolTips.DriveLetterPosition: This setting allows you to choose where drive letters are shown in File Explorer.
GroupsToolTips.ShowTabsInAltTab: This setting allows you to choose whether to show tabs from apps (such as Edge browser tabs) when snapping windows or pressing Alt+Tab.
GroupsToolTips.StartAllAppsView: This setting allows you to change the layout of the 'All Apps' section in the start menu, or hide it entirely. Hiding this section may make it harder to find installed apps on your system. This feature uses policies, which will lock down certain settings.

Values.MMTaskbarModeMainActive: Main taskbar and taskbar where window is open

CombineButtons.Label: Combine taskbar buttons on the main display

CombineMMButtons.Label: Combine taskbar buttons on secondary displays

ClearStart.Label: Remove pinned apps from the start menu

Values.ShowDriveLettersLast: Show drive letters after drive label (Default)
Values.ShowDriveLettersFirst: Show drive letters before drive label
Values.ShowNetworkDriveLettersFirst: Show network drive letters before drive label

ShowTabsInAltTab.Label: Show tabs from apps when snapping or pressing Alt+Tab
